description: A vertical graphic from the U.S. Department of Labor. The top two-thirds is a photograph of a welder at work: a person wearing a dark 'VULCAN'-branded welding helmet, leaning over a workpiece with sparks and smoke and a purple-tinted welding glow around them, inside a shop with warm orange background lighting. A purple-to-navy gradient overlays the image. Across the middle in large display type is the headline 'Trade Schools' (white serif italic) over 'ARE THE FUTURE!' (bold white serif caps). Below sits a white rounded rectangle text card headed with the 'HR Grapevine' logo, quoting an article in black serif text: 'Gen Z, now dubbed the \'toolbelt generation,\' is showing an increased enthusiasm for roles in skilled trades, ranging from carpentry and welding to computer programming. Interest in trade schools has almost doubled since 2017, and Gen Z is at the forefront of that shift. Their motivations paint a picture of a workforce prioritizing hands-on learning, cost-effective education, and faster access to income.' A small DOL seal sits centered at the bottom of the navy background.
notable_text: Trade Schools ARE THE FUTURE!; Gen Z, now dubbed the 'toolbelt generation'
